key_insight: A ~200 µm Al2O3–TiO2 D-gun coating on Ti-6Al-4V significantly improves wear resistance by forming a protective tribo-layer.
engineering_relevance: This work shows that detonation-sprayed ceramic coatings can extend the service life of Ti-6Al-4V components in high-wear aerospace, biomedical, and marine applications.
method: Applied Al2O3–TiO2 coating to Ti-6Al-4V using D-gun processing (O2 4800 SLPH, acetylene 2150 SLPH, stand-off 180 mm).
Measured tribological performance with pin-on-disc testing per ASTM G99 under 40–100 N load, 100–200 rpm, and 20–40 min against EN31 steel.
Built ANN, KNN, and random forest models to predict wear from sliding parameters; random forest performed best.
evidence_signal: Experimental wear testing plus SEM inspection showed lower wear on coated samples, and the random forest model achieved the lowest mean percentage error and mean squared error.
watch_item: The machine-learning models appear to be trained on a limited experimental design, so prediction reliability may be constrained outside the tested load, speed, and time ranges.
paper_title: Tribological enhancement of Al 2 O 3 -TiO 2 D-gun coated Ti-6Al-4V alloy: A machine learning approach
